observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to compute the and provide for depreciation on the PPEs to present faithfully the balances of the said accounts. Thereafter, submit a copy of the lapsing schedule to the Office of the Audit Team for reference.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that:
A demand letter is sent to each accountable officer who has any outstanding cash advance, requiring these officials and employees to liquidate their cash advances immediately; and
The liquidation of cash advances for confidential fund has been submitted to ICFAU, including performance of follow ups to the accountable officers.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to disclose in the Notes to the FS the details of the Loans Payable – Domestic account to provide clarity and sufficient information that is not readily available on the face of the FS as prescribed in IPSAS 1.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal General Services Officer, agree to disclose in the Notes to the FS the required disclosures on the LRNS.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Submit immediately to the Audit Team the AAPSI for the CY 2023 AAR, in accordance with the requirements of Section 99 of the GP of the GAA FY 2023; and,
Designate a responsible person to oversee the preparation, updating, and consolidation of the status of implementation of recommendations, ensuring that in all future periods, the AAPSI is submitted on time.

We recommended the BAC to prepare PMR in the form prescribed by the GPPB and submit to the HOPE for approval and submission to the GPPB in printed and electronic format within 14 days after the end of each semester. The BAC should likewise post the PMR in accordance with E.O. 662, s. 2007, as amended.
observations.managementAction: Strengthen BAC compliance by BAC Chairman Tirso J. Balbin- Designate BAC Focal Person who will oversee the requirements of the PMR submission and Establish a schedule for timely submission.
observations.reason: The BAC was unaware of specific format and posting requirements of the PMR

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management thru the LDDRMO formulate its PSCP to ensure that the agency’s operations can continue and the potential adverse effects arising from disruptive events be mitigated.
observations.managementAction: On going formulation of PSC Plan.
observations.reason: Still in the process of formulation

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management make representation with the Sangguniang Bayan for the creation and installation of the Internal Audit Service (IAS) directly under the Office of the Municipal Mayor to comply with the requirement of AO Nos. 278 and 70 and ensure the quality of performance and compliance with laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The creation and/or opening of the Internal Audit Service (IAS) position has not been implemented due to the lack of funding allocation. A representation shall be made to the newly elected members of the Sangguniang Bayan to explore the possibility of opening the Internal Audit Service (IAS) position.
observations.reason: The creation of the IAS position has not been implemented in due compliance with funding allocation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Assessor convey the issue of the General Revision of Real Property Assessments and Classification to the Provincial level, convincing and enforcing the latter to adhere the Section 219 of RA 7160 to generate additional revenue from the increased collection of real property taxes.
observations.managementAction: Follow up with the newly elected officials regarding the passage of the ordinance. An ordinance on the General Revision of Real Property Assessments and Classification shall be enacted.
observations.reason: The previous Municipal Assessor failed to enact the ordinance on the General Revision of Real Property Assessments and Classification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that LGU allocates funds management thru the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office include in the LDRRM Investment Plan, the establishment of toilets as well as an appropriate storage area in their designated evacuation center, and establish alternative sources of water supply for the evacuation center in compliance with the minimum standards and to further improve the LGUs capabilities to be disaster resilient.
observations.managementAction: The LGU allocates funds for construction of toilet and storage area facilities to designated evacuation centers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to process the titling of the land to prove claims of ownership on the real properties and to safeguard government interest. Allocate funds for the titling of the municipal lots.
observations.managementAction: Request for Funding for the titling and payment of other necessary fees.
observations.reason: There is a lack of funding allocated for the processing of land titling and some required documents were not available because the related transactions were processed by other offices, and the corresponding documents were not submitted to our office

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned municipal officials to comply with Republic Act No. 7743 and DILG MC No. 2017-95, by appropriating the necessary funds in establishing the Municipal Reading Center.
observations.managementAction: 1. Comply with RA 7743 and establish Municipal Library. 2. Issuance of memorandum order to all barangays directing compliance with RA 95. 3. Coordinate with DepEd and other agencies.
observations.reason: 1. Limited funds and space for Municipal Library. 2. Lack of awareness among barangay officials.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LGU’s Inventory Team fast track and complete the PPE inventory count to verify the existence of the LGU’s properties and submit the required report to the Audit Team. Moreover, the inventory report should be reconciled with the accounting and property records. Any discrepancies found should be investigated.
We recommended and Management agreed to direct and ensure that:
a. The Accounting Office apply the criteria on capitalization of PPE and make the necessary adjustments to correct and/or restate the balances of affected accounts and ensure proper classification of assets in the books;
b. GSO Designate update existing PPE and Inventory records and prepare and maintain the additional necessary forms/reports/registries for purposes of record-keeping and installation of controls over semi-expendable properties provided in Section 4 of the Circular. Moreover, use the Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS) for issuance of semi-expandable items to establish monitoring, control, and accountability over them; and
c. Also, to revisit the provisions of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) 17 and other COA issuances, particularly on the recording and disclosure of PPE, including any changes in accounting policies and estimates.
We recommended and Management agreed to provide and effect the necessary adjustment of depreciation to the affected PPE accounts as contemplated in the AG27 of IPSAS 45 and paragraph 2.1 of COA Circular letter No.2004-003 in setting up depreciation.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant present the current portion of Loans Payable - Domestic under the line-item Financial Liabilities in the Statement of Financial Position.
b. Disclose in the NFS the details of the Loans Payable – Domestic account to provide clarity and essential information that are not readily available on the face of the financial statements as prescribed in IPSAS No. 1.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Demand the former Municipal Treasurer to restitute the latter’s undeposited balance and direct the former Assistant Municipal Treasurer to deposit immediately the Cash in Vault balances and thereafter, deposit all collections intact with the authorized depository bank as often as necessary; and
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the variance of the balances presented in the Financial Statements and its corresponding Schedules.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit the monthly report of bills rendered to market occupant which will be made as basis in the accrual and recording of rental income.
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
1. Apply accrual basis as required under Paragraph 7, IPSAS 1 in recording rental income based on the monthly report submitted by the Municipal Treasurer; and,
2. Prepare adjusting entry to revert the recognized rent income from prior years.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Compute and make necessary adjusting entry to capitalize the borrowing costs to present faithfully the balances of the said accounts. Thereafter, submit a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to the Office of the Audit Team for reference.
b. Disclose the amount of borrowing costs capitalized during the year

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Properly disclose the implementation of the above-mentioned resolution along with EO 138, their details, financial, and non-financial effects to the LGU;
b. Revise the account name of the account “Share from Internal Revenue Allotment” to “Share from National Tax Allotment” in compliance with the above-mentioned resolution and EO; and
c. Moving forward, to study constantly the International Federation of Accountants (IFAC) publications, COA accounting rules and regulations and consult with the audit team.

We recommended that Management:
a. Take into consideration the imposition of liquidated damages for those projects that meets the conditions as specified in Item 9.1, Annex E of the 2016 RIRR of RA 9184 (Updated as of 15 April 2023); and
b. Strictly follow and monitor the project timeliness and ensure prompt implementation of the projects. Perform an inspection to ascertain the current status of the project, assess and identify the magnitude of any damage or loss, take necessary measures to ensure the prompt completion of the project, in order for the intended recipients to promptly benefit from the projects.
We recommended and Management agreed to immediately submit the additional documents required as stated above for us to forward the same to the Regional Technical Service of this region.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Issue a demand letter relative to the past due accounts of accountable persons to remind the latter of their duties to liquidate their advances;
b. Properly implement controls over the grant of advances, ensuring that only those who have fully liquidated the amounts previously granted will be allowed to claim any further cash advances.
c. After all the possible measures were undertaken to ascertain the details of the account and proven futile, if warranted, request authority to write off said accounts from the Commission on Audit, following with COA Circular No. 2023-008.
We also recommended that in case of failure to comply with the demand as confirmed by the Municipal Accountant, the Management implement the withholding of salaries or any money due to accountable office with outstanding cash advances, including filing the appropriate legal sanctions according to Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code for failure to render accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Direct the HRMO to submit a proposal to the Sangguniang Bayan for the enactment of Ordinance approving the creation of the Plantilla position for the IAS.
b. Otherwise, and in the meantime, designate personnel/s to concurrently serve as Internal Audit Service/Unit and direct the IAS Officer to align its activities with the provisions of PGIAM while carrying out its mandated functions.

We recommended that the LDRRM Officer:
a. Observe the proper planning of the PPAs to maximize the utilization of the allocated funds;
b. Regularly and continuously monitor the status of the PPAs in the LDRRMF Investment Plan and ensure its implementation within the prescribed timeline; and
c. Regularly evaluate the effectiveness and feasibility of the LDRRMP and determine whether they are in accordance with other national and local planning programs and policies on disaster risk reduction.

We recommended and Management agreed to exercise sound judgment in the utilization of the LDRRMF and hereon forward stop the practice of charging expenditures that are not fully aligned/unrelated with the provision of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and prioritize the implementation of PAPs related to disaster prevention and recovery.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct and ensure that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer effect without delay the transfer of the much-needed funds relative to the 25% share on RPT collections and municipal aid to the component Barangays as mandated.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct and ensure that the MSWDO:
a. Prepare clear-cut policies and guidelines for approval of the Municipal Mayor which shall include the criteria/factors used and prescribed rates which will be the basis in the proper evaluation and grant of requests for assistance and subsequently coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan for the adoption these guidelines.
b. Adequately evaluate all applications for assistance against the established guidelines to be set by the LGU, ensuring that all the parameters set in the guidelines are sufficiently met.
We recommended and Management agreed to properly apply the guidelines on the grant of mid-year bonus so that only those who are entitled to such amounts be paid.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to ensure the maximum utilization of GAD funds through the implementation of all planned GAD-related programs and projects during the year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct and ensure that the GAD Focal Person prepare and establish the GAD budget by attribution, integrating the GAD perspective in major programs as a means of gradually increasing the allocation on GAD Budget and the gender responsiveness of the agency.
We recommended and Management agreed to send invitation to the Audit Team and other NGOs in conducting bidding activities as required by the revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended and Management agreed to exclusively use the CENRR for expenses amounting ₱300 or less in lieu of ORs, and appropriately employ other forms as prescribed by the circumstances outlined in COA Circular No. 2021-001.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Withhold five percent (5%) creditable taxes based on gross income for the professional fees rendered as prescribed in the Revenue Regulations (RR) No. 11-2018.
b. File and remit the same withheld taxes to the BIR within the due date.

We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office should see to it that net take home pay of employees shall not be less than P5,000.00. In the event that total authorized deductions shall reduce net take home pay to less than Five Thousand Pesos (P5,000.00), deductions shall be made in accordance with the order of preference under Section 55 of 2022 GAA and employee must be advised to cease the loan application.
observations.managementAction: A minimum monthly take-home pay of not less than ₱5,000 shall be strictly observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Solid Waste Management Board effectively and efficiently implement the solid waste management programs, projects, and activities embodied in the SWMP, especially the construction of sanitary landfill category 1.
observations.managementAction: The construction of the Sanitary Landfill Category 1 has been effectively and efficiently implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management create an Inventory committee composed of an adequate number of members to be able to complete the physical inventory count. The inventory committee shall have at least one member each from the Accounting and Treasury Office. They are required to submit the RPCPPE on or before January 31 of each year, copy furnished the COA Auditor concerned

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to submit to COA the mentioned 43 canceled checks for validation and henceforth, ensure that all cancelled checks are attached to the RCI and reported chronologically with the other checks issued with the word “Cancelled” clearly indicated therein.
We recommended the following properly present the CBMS database to the audit team.

We recommended that the practice of granting cash advances to national government officials were in this situation, the DILG-Officer, be stopped because he is not bonded. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant should issue a demand letter relative to the past due account to remind the accountable person of his duty to liquidate his cash advance.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer and Assistant Municipal Treasurer to deposit immediately the Cash in Vault balance of P7,356,706.76 and thereafter, deposit all collections intact with the authorized depository bank as often as necessary.
We recommended that municipal Accountant issue a demand letter relative to the past due accounts of accountable persons to remind the latter of their duties to liquidate their advances;

We recommended that management thru the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office include in the LDRRM Investment Plan, the establishment of toilets as well as an appropriate storage area in their designated evacuation center, and establish alternative sources of water supply for the evacuation center in compliance with the minimum standards and to further improve the LGUs capabilities to be disaster resilient.

We recommended that management directs the General Services Officer (GSO) and Municipal Engineer to submit promptly all contracts with their complete supporting documents to the Audit Team for review within five days from the execution of the contract in compliance with the existing regulations.

We recommended that concerned LGU officials and employees should always be aware and committed to implement all applicable laws, rules and regulations in the granting as well as the utilization of cash advances to avoid possible disciplinary actions or sanctions in the future for non-compliance thereof.

We recommended that Management make representation with the Sangguniang Bayan for the creation and installation of the Internal Audit Service (IAS) directly under the Office of the Municipal Mayor to comply with the requirement of AO Nos. 278 and 70.

We recommended the Management to process the titling of the land to prove claims of ownership on the real properties and to safeguard government interest. Allocate funds for the titling of the municipal lots.
We recommended the concerned municipal officials to comply with Republic Act No. 7743 and DILG MC No. 2017-95, by appropriating the necessary funds in establishing the Municipal Reading Center.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive and OIC-SWMFP involve closely the barangay officials in solid wastes management and activate the Barangay Solid Waste Management Committees for the strict enforcement and continual implementation of the existing environmental laws and consider extending financial assistance, resources, and technology to the 18 barangays which have remained non-compliant in establishing MRFs.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer timely submit the related RCIs, RODs, RCDs and supporting documents for all funds to the Municipal Accountant for the timely recording in the books of accounts and preparation of the trial balances, financial statements, bank reconciliation statements and other related periodic reports for submission to the Auditor and other government regulatory bodies.
We recommended that the concerned LGU officials and employees safeguard government resources and prevent loss, misappropriation and misuse of government funds and resources

We recommended that management to:


        Create an Inventory  committee  composed  of  adequate  number  of
        members to be able to complete the physical inventory  count.  The
        inventory committee shall have at least one member each  from  the
        Accounting and Treasury Office. They are required  to  submit  the
        RPCPPE on or before January 31 of each year,  copy  furnished  the
        COA Auditor concerned; and


        Check the condition of the  property.  Any  damage,  obsolete  and
        unserviceable property shall be  reported  in  the  Inventory  and
        Inspection Report of  Unserviceable  Property  (IIRUP)  and  Waste
        Materials Report and immediately request for disposal. Lastly, the
        inventory reports should be reconciled with the accounting records
        to determine the correctness of the property records.

We recommended that reconciliation of the affected accounts  should
       be made and prepare appropriate adjusting entries.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be directed to provide
       proper adjustment of depreciation to the PPEs account affected  and
       to submit the lapsing schedule of depreciation.

We recommended that the Municipal  Accountant  review  the  Journal
       Entry Voucher (JEV) and postings, including  the  footings  of  the
       accounts for possible errors and discrepancies in the recording  of
       transactions  in  the  books  of  accounts  of  the  LGU,  and   to
       immediately adjust the affected accounts for reliable and  accurate
       reporting. A copy of JEV shall be furnished to the  office  of  the
       auditor.

We recommended that concerned LGU officials and employees headed by
       the LCE himself fully implement the provisions of COA Circular  No.
       2013 - 004 dated January 30, 2013, on all its on-going and/or about
       to start Programs/Projects/Activities (PPAs) to inform  and  notify
       the public of the essential features/significance/elements  of  the
       LGU’s PPAs as required per regulation for purposes of  transparency
       and good governance.

We recommended that Item V of COA Circular 89-296 dated January 27,
       1989, be observed in the disposal  of  unserviceable  property  and
       other assets. Management  shall  prepare  and  submit an  Inventory
       Report showing the itemized list and complete  description  of  the
       assets, an Appraisal Report showing the  appraised  values  of  the
       assets, prepared by an in-house and/or independent appraiser, and a
       Disposal procedure adopted.

We recommended that management thru the BAC Secretariat  furnish
           the Auditor with a copy  of  perfected  contracts  and  purchase
           orders within  five  working  days  from  the  executions  of  a
           contract together with the supporting  documents  to  facilitate
           the  review  and  evaluation  process.  Likewise,  request   for
           technical inspection of completed projects must be submitted  as
           required.

We recommended that all collecting officers prepare and submit  the
       RAAF along with the  RIS  if  any  on  a  monthly  basis  for  easy
       monitoring of the movement and status  of  his  accountability  for
       accountable forms in compliance with Section 98 of  the  Government
       Accounting and Auditing Manual (GAAM) Vol. 1.

We recommended that management prepare and submit the Statement  of
       Receipts and Expenditures to liquidate the funds received  from  DA
       for proper accounting and reporting.

We recommended that Management adopt and maintain the perpetual
           inventory method and adhere to Section 121 of NGAS in recording
           for proper accounting and reporting.

We recommended that management require the contractor of future
           infrastructure projects to post bank guarantee bond  equivalent
           to 10% contract price  in  lieu  of  the  10%  retention  money
           purposely to cover all defects that may be detected,  including
           maintenance  costs  for  one  year  after  project  completion.
           Otherwise, 10% retention money should be retained in the  final
           payment of the project contract until such time that the defect
           liability period of one year expires.

We recommended that the municipal mayor directs the  GAD  Focal
           Person to maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through the
           implementation of GAD-related programs and projects  to  attain
           the objective for which funds were provided.

We recommended that management utilize the  20%  DF  and  ensure
           that all programmed projects are  implemented  during  the  year
           pursuant to the intent of DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular
           No. 1 dated November  1,  2020.

We recommended the following:

            a. The BAC Chairman directs the BAC Secretariat to send  written
               notice to losing bidders notifying the  bidding  results  and
               the recommendation of award of the contract.


            b. The BAC Chairman directs the BAC Secretariat to  comply  with
               the posting requirements on all its procurement.  Notices  of
               Award and Proceed, and the approved contract be posted in the
               PhilGEPS website and website of  the  municipality,  if  any,
               within the required period as required by  the  2016  Revised
               IRR of R.A. No. 9184


            c. The BAC strictly comply  with  the  procedures  on  receipts,
               opening and preliminary examination of bids as  provided  for
               in the Procurement Manual for LGUs and the 2016  Revised  IRR
               of R.A. No. 9184.

We recommended that:

            a. Municipal Accountant issue a demand letter relative to  the
               past due accounts of  accountable  persons  to  remind  the
               latter of their duties to liquidate their advances;


            b. In case of failure to comply, the LGU should implement  the
               withholding of salaries or any money due to them, including
               appropriate legal sanctions according to Article 218 of the
               Revised Penal Code for failure to render accounts; and


            c.  After  all  the  possible  measures  were  undertaken   to
               ascertain the details of the  account  and  proven  futile,
               request authority to  write  off  said  accounts  from  the
               Commission on Audit, following with COA Circular No.  2016-
               005 dated Dec.19, 2016.

We recommended that Management thru  the  LDDRMO  formulate  its
           PSCP to ensure that the agency’s operations can continue and the
           potential adverse effects  arising  from  disruptive  events  be
           mitigated.

We recommended that the Local Chief  Executive  and  the  Solid
           Waste Management Board effectively  and  efficiently  implement
           the solid waste management programs, projects,  and  activities
           embodied in the SWMP, especially the construction  of  sanitary
           landfill category 1.

We recommended that Management  make  representation  with  the
           Sangguniang Bayan for the  creation  and  installation  of  the
           Internal Audit Service (IAS) directly under the Office  of  the
           Municipal Mayor to comply with the requirement of AO  Nos.  278
           and 70 and ensure the quality  of  performance  and  compliance
           with laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended  that  management  observe  the  procedures  and
           procurement process  of  RA  9184  in  the  leasing  venue  for
           seminars and workshops. Moreover, we recommend the  accountable
           officer justify how  the  abovementioned  transaction  did  not
           undergo public bidding.

We recommended that:

          a) The Municipal Treasurer to send a written notice to the  payees
             one month before the check becomes stale.


          b) For  improperly  reported  stale  checks,  the  Office  of  the
             Municipal Treasurer and the Office of the Municipal Accountant,
             provide evidence that the checks are not outstanding; that they
             have been voided and no obligations exist.


          c) The  Municipal  Accountant  to  make  the  necessary  adjusting
             entries for stale-dated checks as follows:

            a. For stale checks over 6 months but not over 2 years –


                 CIB-LC,CA                    P xxxxx
                            Accounts Payable                  P xxxxx


            b. For stale checks over 2 years –


                 CIB-LC,CA                    P xxxxx
                        Prior Years Adjustment*                     P xxxxx
                         (*to represent surplus adjustment)

We recommended that the Municipal Assessor convey the issue  of
           the   General  Revision  of  Real  Property   Assessments   and
           Classification  to  the  Provincial   level,   convincing   and
           enforcing the latter to adhere the Section 219 of  RA  7160  to
           generate additional revenue from the  increased  collection  of
           real property taxes.

We recommended that the LCE  directs  the  Local  Accountant  to
           comply/adhere with the regulation by submitting  the  Report  on
           the Sources and  Utilization  of  Disaster  Risk  Reduction  and
           Management Fund for the auditor to properly review  and  monitor
           the  charges  made  on  the  DRRMF.   Moreover,   disclose   the
           unexpended balance of the  LDRRMF  in  the  Notes  to  Financial
           Statements.
We recommended that reconciliation of the affected accounts should be made and prepare appropriate adjusting entries.

We recommended that Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant coordinate each other for the strict compliance of the above cited regulation for reliable and accurate account balance in the financial statements as of any given period of time. Instruct the Municipal Treasure’s Office to come up with a certified list of taxpayers and furnish the same to the Municipal Accountant. The certified list of taxpayers will be the basis of the Accountant in setting up the real Property Tax/Special Education Tax receivable and deferred income at the beginning of the year.
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer to send a written notice to the payees one month before the check becomes stale.

We recommended that alternative methods of procurement shall be resorted only under the extraordinary circumstances provided for in Section 53 and other instances specified in the IRR of R.A. No. 9184.  In all instances, ensure that the most advantageous price for the LGU is obtained.

We recommended that management stop granting honorarium to the employees who are not in the list of specified national government officials entitled to receive honoraria due to the absence of a legal basis.
We recommended that management use the certification of expenses not requiring receipts for expenses P300.00 and below and RER for expenses exceeding P300.00.

We recommended that management instruct their canvassers to require the prospective suppliers to indicate the brand names and other detailed item specifications. Moreover, direct the General Services Unit to prepare all the purchase orders entered into by the LGU and accomplishes properly the POs including the brand names and other detailed specifications of goods to be procured.

We recommended that the practice of granting cash advance to an elected official for Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) other than Traveling Expenses should be stopped to conform to the existing rules and regulations.  Their tenure is fixed and limited by operations of law; thus, they leave the office when their terms of office expire or when they lose in their bids for re-election leaving their unsettled cash advances unaccounted for.

We recommended that the concerned job order workers be required to submit their daily time records and individual accomplishment reports duly verified and signed by their immediate supervisors in adherence to Section 4 (6) of PD 1445 and Section 1.2.1 of COA Circular 2012-001. This is to ensure that the paid wages of the job order workers were rendered in accordance with their assigned tasks and their services were truly necessary.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant issue a certification that the previous cash advances have been liquidated or settled in compliance with section 1.1.4.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. Certification written in the face of Disbursement Voucher is already sufficient.
We recommended the following written explanation of why the paid website is not yet functioning and operational.

We recommended the property officer maintain efficient property management by following the prescribed rules and procedures in property recording and regularly renew the PAR. Moreover, we also recommend the Accounting Office to maintain an updated PPE ledger card reconciled with the property cards of the GSO.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the DV together with the supporting document/s schedule for the return of the unused balance and the Municipal Treasurer issue the corresponding check payable to the Source Agency.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit the Statement of Comparison Statement of Budget and Actual Amount for CYs 2018 and 2019.

We recommended that management thru the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office include in the LDRRM Investment Plan, the establishment of toilets as well as an appropriate storage area in their designated evacuation center, and establish alternative sources of water supply for the evacuation center in compliance with the minimum standards and to further improve the LGUs capabilities to be disaster resilient.

We recommended that management directs the General Services Officer (GSO) and Municipal Engineer to submit promptly all contracts with their complete supporting documents to the Audit Team for review within five days from the execution of the contract in compliance with the existing regulations.

We recommended that concerned LGU officials and employees should always be aware and committed to implement all applicable laws, rules and regulations in the granting as well as the utilization of cash advances to avoid possible disciplinary actions or sanctions in the future for non-compliance thereof. 

Issue a demand letter relative to the past due accounts to remind the accountable persons of their duties to liquidate their advances.

We recommended that Management make representation with the Sangguniang Bayan for the creation and installation of the Internal Audit Service (IAS) directly under the Office of the Municipal Mayor to comply with the requirement of AO Nos. 278 and 70.

We recommended the Management to process the titling of the land to prove claims of ownership on the real properties and to safeguard government interest. Allocate funds for the titling of the municipal lots.

We recommended that the concerned municipal officials to comply with Republic Act No. 7743 and DILG MC No. 2017-95, by appropriating the necessary funds in establishing the Municipal Reading Center. 

The municipal mayor shall also supervise the Punong Barangay’s compliance in establishing the Barangay Reading Center

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and OIC- SWMFP involve closely the barangay officials in solid wastes management and activate the Barangay Solid Waste Management Committees for the strict enforcement and continual implementation of the existing environmental laws and consider extending financial assistance, resources, and technology to the 18 barangays which have remained non-compliant in establishing MRFs.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer timely submit the related RCIs, RODs, RCDs and supporting documents for all funds to the Municipal Accountant for the timely recording in the books of accounts and preparation of the trial balances, financial statements, bank reconciliation statements and other related periodic reports for submission to the Auditor and other government regulatory bodies.
We recommended that the concerned LGU officials and employees safeguard government resources and prevent loss, misappropriation and misuse of government funds and resources

We recommended that management
Create an Inventory committee composed of an adequate number of members to be able to complete the physical inventory count. The inventory committee shall have at least one member each from the Accounting and Treasury Office. They are required to submit the RPCPPE on or before January 31 of each year, copy furnished the COA Auditor concerned; and
Check the condition of the property. Any damage, obsolete and unserviceable property shall be reported in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and Waste Materials Report and immediately request for disposal. Lastly, the inventory reports should be reconciled with the accounting records to determine the correctness of the property records
We recommended that reconciliation of the affected accounts should be made and prepare appropriate adjusting entries.

We recommended that Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant coordinate each other for the strict compliance of the above cited regulation for reliable and accurate account balance in the financial statements as of any given period of time. Instruct the Municipal Treasures Office to come up with a certified list of taxpayers and furnish the same to the Municipal Accountant. The certified list of taxpayers will be the basis of the Accountant in setting up the real Property Tax/Special Education Tax receivable and deferred income at the beginning of the year.

We recommended that –
The Municipal Treasurer to send a written notice to the payees one month before the check becomes stale.
For improperly reported stale checks, the Office of the Municipal Treasurer and the Office of the Municipal Accountant, provide evidence that the checks are not outstanding; that they have been voided and no obligations exist.
The Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries for stale-dated checks as follows:
For stale checks over 6 months but not over 2 years –
CIB-LC, CA			P xxxxx
Accounts Payable			P xxxxx
For stale checks over 2 years –
CIB-LC, CA			P xxxxx
Prior Years Adjustment*			P xxxxx
(*to represent surplus adjustment)

We recommended that alternative methods of procurement shall be resorted only under the extraordinary circumstances provided for in Section 53 and other instances specified in the IRR of R.A. No. 9184.  In all instances, ensure that the most advantageous price for the LGU is obtained.

We recommended that management stop granting honorarium to the employees who are not in the list of specified national government officials entitled to receive honoraria due to the absence of a legal basis.
We recommended that management use the certification of expenses not requiring receipts for expenses P300.00 and below and RER for expenses exceeding P300.00.

We recommended that management instruct their canvassers to require the prospective suppliers to indicate the brand names and other detailed item specifications. Moreover, direct the General Services Unit to prepare all the purchase orders entered into by the LGU and accomplishes properly the POs including the brand names and other detailed specifications of goods to be procured to serve the purpose for which the purchase order was prescribed.

We recommended that the practice of granting cash advance to an elected official for Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) other than Traveling Expenses should be stopped to conform to the existing rules and regulations.  Their tenure is fixed and limited by operations of law; thus, they leave the office when their terms of office expire or when they lose in their bids for re-election leaving their unsettled cash advances unaccounted for.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to submit to COA the mentioned 43 canceled checks for validation and henceforth, ensure that all cancelled checks are attached to the RCI and reported chronologically with the other checks issued with the word “Cancelled” clearly indicated therein.

We recommended that the concerned job order workers be required to submit their daily time records and individual accomplishment reports duly verified and signed by their immediate supervisors in adherence to Section 4 (6) of PD 1445 and Section 1.2.1 of COA Circular 2012-001.  This is to ensure that the paid wages of the job order workers were rendered in accordance with their assigned tasks and their services were truly necessary.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant issue a certification that the previous cash advances have been liquidated or settled in compliance with section 1.1.4.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. Certification written in the face of Disbursement Voucher is already sufficient.

We recommended the following:
Written explanation of why the paid website is not yet functioning and operational.
Written explanation of why the said website is handled by the DILG-Officer when in fact it should have been manned by the LGU Personnel.
Written explanation why the requisitioning officer’s signatory in the PR and Abstract of Bid/Quotation are not signed by the same person.
Proof that the said procurement undergone two (2) failed public bidding before resorting to Small Value Procurement
Notify the audit team that the website is already running online.

We recommended the following:
Properly present the CBMS database to the audit team.
Written explanation why the said database is handled by the DILG-Officer when in fact it should have been manned by the LGU Personnel.
Written explanation why there is non-withholding of Creditable Value Added Tax.
Proof that the said procurement has undergone two (2) failed public bidding before resorting to Small Value Procurement as an alternative mode of procurement.
Legal basis relative to the procurement of consultancy services for the implementation of CBMS.

We recommended that the practice of granting cash advances to national government officials were in this situation, the DILG-Officer, be stopped because he is not bonded. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant should issue a demand letter relative to the past due account to remind the accountable person of his duty to liquidate his cash advance.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer and Assistant Municipal Treasurer to deposit immediately the Cash in Vault balance of P7,356,706.76 and thereafter, deposit all collections intact with the authorized depository bank as often as necessary.

We recommended the property officer maintain efficient property management by following the prescribed rules and procedures in property recording and regularly renew the PAR. Moreover, we also recommend the Accounting Office maintain an updated PPE ledger card reconciled with the property cards of the GSO.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the DV together with the supporting document/schedule for the return of the unused balance and the Municipal Treasurer issue the corresponding check payable to the Source Agency.

We recommended that:
Municipal Accountant issue a demand letter relative to the past due accounts of accountable persons to remind the latter of their duties to liquidate their advances;
In case of failure to comply, the LGU should implement the withholding of salaries or any money due to them, including appropriate legal sanctions according to Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code for failure to render accounts; and
After all the possible measures were undertaken to ascertain the details of the account and proven futile, request authority to write off said accounts from the Commission on Audit, following with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated Dec.19, 2016.

We recommended that management maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through the implementation of GAD-related programs and projects in order to attain the objective for which the funds were provided.

We recommended that management not to authorize deductions from the salaries of the employees that will reduce their net take home pay below P 5,000.00. Deductions from salaries should be prioritized as stated in Section 52 of RA 11260.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit the Statement of Comparison Statement of Budget and Actual Amount for CYs 2018 and 2019.
We recommended that management follow the guidelines in the preparation and utilization of the GAD budget as provided in the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular 2013-01.

We recommended that LGU-Olutanga ask for the refund of the P100,000.00 donation for the procurement of service vehicle for the use of the Division Superintendent of DepEd, ZS from LGU-Titay, Zamboanga Sibugay.

We recommended that Management implement and utilize the LCPC Fund to its programs, projects and activities (PPAs). Maximize the allocated LCPC Fund to the identified PPAs provided for in DILG MC No 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012

Create a team to conduct an immediate physical count of all its PPE. This committee may be composed of the heads or representatives from the Office of the Municipal Treasurer, the Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Engineer, and a representative from the Office of the Municipal Mayor. They are required to submit the RPCPPE on or before January 31 of each year, copy furnished the COA Auditor concerned pursuant to Section 124 of NGAS Manual, Volume I and Section 490 of GAAM, Volume I;

We recommended that reconciliation of the affected accounts be made and prepare appropriate adjusting entries.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers/supporting schedules for all asset and liability accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management make representation with the Sangguniang Bayan for the creation and installation of the Internal Audit Service (IAS) directly under the Office of the Municipal Mayor to comply with the requirement of AO Nos. 278 and 70 and ensure the quality of performance and compliance with laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended the Management process the titling of the land to prove claims of ownership on the real properties and to safeguard government interest. Allocate funds for the titling of the municipal lots.
observations.managementAction: Management is on the process of titling of lands.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the concerned municipal officials to comply with Republic Act No. 7743 and DILG MC No. 2017-95, by appropriating the necessary funds in establishing the Municipal Reading Center. The municipal mayor shall also supervise the Punong Barangay’s compliance in establishing the Barangay Reading Center.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and OIC-SWMFP involve closely the barangay officials in solid wastes management and activate the Barangay Solid Waste Management Committees for the strict enforcement and continual implementation of the existing environmental laws and consider extending financial assistance, resources, and technology to the 18 barangays which have remained non-compliant in establishing MRFs. Moreover, continuously intensify information campaign and educate the constituents on the necessity of establishment of MRF in every barangay.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer timely submit the related RCIs, RODs, RCDs and supporting documents for all funds to the Municipal Accountant for the timely recording in the books of accounts and preparation of the trial balances, financial statements, bank reconciliation statements and other related periodic reports for submission to the Auditor and other government regulatory bodies.

observations.recommendation: Management may coordinate properly with the Environmental Management Bureau (EMB) for the implementation of the latter’s recommendation on the matter as an alternative method pending the accumulation of the necessary resources/funds for the full implementation of the said law requiring the construction of a Sanitary Landfill for the LGU’s waste management disposal method.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation


   5. We recommended Management to:

          1. Create a team to conduct an immediate physical count of all its
             property, plant and equipment. This committee may  be  composed
             of  the  heads  or  representatives  from  the  Office  of  the
             Municipal Treasurer, the Municipal  Accountant,  the  Municipal
             Engineer, and a representative from the Office of the Municipal
             Mayor. They are required to submit  the  RPCPPE  on  or  before
             January 31  of  each  year,  copy  furnished  the  COA  Auditor
             concerned pursuant to Section 124 of NGAS Manual, Volume I  and
             Section 490 of GAAM, Volume I;


          2.  Further,  the  committee  should  be  required  to  check  the
             condition  of  the   property.   Any   damage,   obsolete   and
             unserviceable property shall be reported in the  Inventory  and
             Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP)  and  Waste
             Materials Report and immediately request for disposal.  Lastly,
             the inventory reports should be reconciled with the  accounting
             records to determine the correctness of the  property  records;
             and


          3. Require each Department Head to conduct every year  a  physical
             inventory count of all properties under  their  office  and  to
             submit inventory report to  the  inventory  committee  for  the
             random validation of the existence of the property.

Recommendation


   6. We recommended that reconciliation of the affected accounts  be  made.
      The municipal accountant shall draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV)  and
      record the adjustments. A copy of the JEV shall be  furnished  to  the
      office of the auditor.

Recommendation


   4. We recommended that the  Municipal  Accountant  prepare  and  maintain
      subsidiary ledgers/supporting schedules for all  asset  and  liability
      accounts.

Recommendation


   6. We recommended that –


          1. The Municipal Treasurer send a written notice to the payees one
             month before the check becomes stale as required in Section  59
             of the  NGAS  Manual.   Stale  checks  shall  be  reported  and
             attached to the RCI prepared at the period of cancellation.


          2. For  improperly  reported  stale  checks,  the  Office  of  the
             Municipal Treasurer and the Office of the Municipal Accountant,
             provide evidence that the checks are not outstanding; that they
             have been voided and no obligations exist.


          3. The  Municipal  Accountant  to  make  the  necessary  adjusting
             entries for stale-dated checks as follows:


            a. For stale checks over 6 months but not over 2 years –


                 CIB-LC,CA                    P xxxxx
                           Accounts Payable                  P xxxxx


            b. For stale checks over 2 years –


                 CIB-LC,CA                    P xxxxx
                        Prior Years Adjustment*                     P xxxxx
                         (*to represent surplus adjustment)

Recommendation


   6. We recommended that management thru the Local Disaster Risk  Reduction
      Management  Office  include  in  the  LDRRM   Investment   Plan,   the
      establishment of toilets as well as an  appropriate  storage  area  in
      their designated evacuation center, and establish alternative  sources
      of water supply for the  evacuation  center  in  compliance  with  the
      minimum standards and to further improve the LGUs capabilities  to  be
      disaster resilient.

Recommendation


   7. We recommended that management maximize the  utilization  of  the  GAD
      funds through the implementation of GAD-related programs and  projects
      in order to attain the objective for which the funds were provided.

Recommendation


   11. We recommended that management directs the General Services  Officer
       (GSO) and Municipal Engineer to submit promptly all  contracts  with
       their complete supporting documents to the  Audit  Team  for  review
       within five days from the execution of the  contract  in  compliance
       with the  existing  regulations.  Likewise,  request  for  technical
       inspection of completed projects must be submitted as required.

Recommendation


   4. We recommended that management not to authorize deductions from  the
      salaries of the employees that will reduce their net take  home  pay
      below P 5,000.00. Deductions from salaries should be prioritized  as
      stated in Section 52 of RA 11260.

Recommendation


   5. We recommended that  concerned  LGU  officials  and  employees  should
      always be aware and committed to implement all applicable laws,  rules
      and regulations in the granting as well as the utilization  of  cash
      advances to avoid possible disciplinary actions or  sanctions  in  the
      future for non-compliance thereof. Issue a demand letter  relative  to
      the past due accounts to  remind  the  accountable  persons  of  their
      duties to liquidate their advances. We also require the submission  of
      copies of the transmittal letters duly received by COA Central  Office
      on the liquidation of the intelligence fund. With regards to the over-
      liquidation amounts, notify the accountable officers with their claims
      and make  the  necessary  adjustments  if  claims are not warranted.

Recommendation


     5. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the  Municipal
        Treasurer to strictly observe the following suggested measures:


           1. Deposit all collections intact with the authorized depository
              bank as often as necessary; and


           2. Stop immediately the practice  of  holding  large  amount  of
              undeposited collections depriving the  municipality  and  its
              constituents immediate delivery of basic services.

Recommendation


     4. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit the
        Statement of Comparison Statement of Budget and Actual  Amount  for
        CYs 2018 and 2019.
We recommended that management follow the guidelines in the preparation and utilization of the GAD budget as provided in the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular 2013-01.

We recommended that LGU-Olutanga ask for the refund of the P100,000.00 donation for the procurement of service vehicle for the use of the Division Superintendent of DepEd, ZS from LGU-Titay, Zamboanga Sibugay.

      request  for  disposal.  Lastly,  the  inventory  reports  should   be

   a. Direct the engineering department to submit the  necessary  supporting

observations.recommendation:
We  recommend  that  concerned  management  officials  to  religiously
implement the above cited regulations on the conduct  of  periodic  physical
inventory of the LGU’s  PPE  as  well  as  to  appropriately  reconcile  the
related account balances recorded in the books of accounts.

We  recommend  that  the  Municipal  Treasurer  and   the   Municipal
Accountant to coordinate each other for the strict compliance of the  above
cited regulation for reliable and accurate account balance in the financial
statements as of any  given  time.  Instruct  the  accountant  to  use  the
Municipal Treasurer’s Office certified list of taxpayers showing the amount
due for the year in setting up the real Property Tax/Special Education  Tax
receivable and at the beginning of the year as required under  Section  20,
Volume 1 of NGAS Manual.

We recommend that concerned management officials to exert all  efforts
to collect the receivable accounts or to  appropriately  correct  or  adjust
the accounts for possible errors for fair presentation of  the  accounts  in
the financial statements.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to  direct  the  Municipal
Accountant to strictly observe the rules and regulations on the  preparation
of the financial statements.

We recommend that the concerned municipal officials to  comply  with
Republic Act No. 7743 and DILG  MC  No.  2017-95,  by  appropriating  the
necessary  funds  in  establishing  the  Municipal  Reading  Center.  The
municipal mayor shall also supervise the Punong Barangay’s compliance  in
establishing the Barangay Reading Center.

We recommend that the concerned municipal officials  to  comply  COA
Cir.  No.  2017-019  dated  October  19,  2017-12-05,   by   immediately
withdrawing the deposits made to PNB and depositing  the  same  with  the
above listed authorized depository banks.

We  recommend  that  management  to  follow  the  guidelines  in   the
utilization of the LDRRMF as provided in COA Circular No. 2012-02.

We recommend that concerned LGU officials and employees should  always
be  aware  and  committed  to  implement  all  applicable  laws,  rules  and
regulations in the granting as well as the utilization of cash  advances  to
avoid possible disciplinary actions or sanctions  in  the  future  for  non-
compliance thereof. Issue a demand letter relative to the past due  accounts
to remind the  accountable  persons  of  their  duties  to  liquidate  their
advances. We also require  the  submission  of  copies  of  the  transmittal
letters duly received by COA  Central  Office  on  the  liquidation  of  the
intelligence fund.

We recommend that the management directs the General Services  Officer
(GSO) and Municipal Engineer to submit promptly  all  contracts  with  their
complete supporting documents to the Audit Team for review within five  days
from  the  execution  of  the  contract  in  compliance  with  the  existing
regulations.  Likewise,  request  for  technical  inspection  of   completed
projects must be submitted as required.

We recommend that the  Local  Chief  Executive  and  OIC-  SWMFP  to
involve closely the barangay officials in  solid  wastes  management  and
activate the Barangay Solid Waste Management Committees  for  the  strict
enforcement and continual implementation of  the  existing  environmental
8laws  and  consider  extending  financial  assistance,  resources,   and
technology to the 18  barangays  which  have  remained  non-compliant  in
establishing MRFs. Moreover, continuously intensify information  campaign
and educate the constituents on the necessity of establishment of MRF  in
every barangay.

We recommend that  the  Municipal  Treasurer  to  timely  submit  the
related RCIs, RODs, RCDs and supporting  documents  for  all  funds  to  the
Municipal Accountant for the timely recording in the books of  accounts  and
preparation   of   the   trial   balances,   financial   statements,    bank
reconciliation statements and other related periodic reports for  submission
to the Auditor and other government regulatory bodies.

We recommend that management not  to  authorize  deductions  from  the
salaries of the   employees that will reduce their net take home  pay  below
P 4,000.00. Deductions from salaries should be prioritized as  shown  above.

We recommend the
municipal accountant
to review the JEVs and
postings including the
footings of the
accounts for possible
errors and
discrepancies in the
recording of
transactions in the
books of accounts of
the LGU and to effect
immediate adjustments
of the affected
accounts for reliable
and accurate
reporting.

We recommend that the
Municipal Treasurer to
timely submit the
above cited RCIs,
RODs, ROCs and the
related supporting
documents for all
funds to the Municipal
Accountant for the
timely recording in
the books of account
and timely preparation
by the latter of the
financial statements,
bank reconciliation
statements and other
related periodic
reports for submission
to the Auditor and
other government
regulatory bodies.

observations.recommendation:
We  recommend  that  concerned  management  officials  to  religiously
implement the above cited regulations on the conduct  of  periodic  physical
count of property plant and equipment as well as to appropriately  reconcile
the related account balances recorded in the books of accounts of the  local
government unit.

We recommend the municipal accountant to review the JEVs and  postings
including  the  footings  of  the   accounts   for   possible   errors   and
discrepancies in the recording of transactions in the books of  accounts  of
the LGU and to effect immediate adjustments of  the  affected  accounts  for
reliable and accurate reporting.

We recommend the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant  to
coordinate each  other  for  the  strict  compliance  of  the  above  cited
regulation for reliable and  accurate  account  balance  in  the  financial
statements as of any given period of time.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer to timely  submit  the  above
cited RCIs, RODs, ROCs and the related supporting documents  for  all  funds
to the Municipal Accountant  for  the  timely  recording  in  the  books  of
account and timely preparation by the latter of  the  financial  statements,
bank reconciliation  statements  and  other  related  periodic  reports  for
submission to the Auditor and other government regulatory bodies.

We recommend that municipal mayor to ensure that all LGU  owned  motor
vehicles are properly and timely registered with the LTO  and  are  properly
insured with the GSIS as required by law and regulation.

We  recommend  that  concerned  LGU   officials   and   personnel   to
appropriately  act  on  the  noted   deficiencies/discrepancies   to   avoid
disallowances in audit.

We recommend that concerned LGU officials and employees headed by  the
LCE himself to fully implement the provisions of COA  Circular  No.  2013  -
004 dated January 30, 2013  on  all  its  on-going  and/or  about  to  start
Programs/Projects/Activities (PPAs) to inform and notify the public  of  the
essential features/significance/elements of the LGU’s PPAs as  required  per
regulation for purposes of transparency and good  governance.   Furthermore,
hold or suspend any payment related to all contractors who failed to  comply
with this regulation to make them  aware  of  their  responsibilities  after
entering into contract with the government in undertaking  and  implementing
government projects.

We recommend that the property officer  to  renew  all  Acknowledgment
Receipt for Equipment in accordance with  the  instructions  under  the  COA
Handbook on Property and Supply Management System.

We recommend that concerned management  officials  and  employees
to  implement  strictly  the  above  cited  regulation  to  avoid   possible
suspensions in audit or facing the consequences for its non-compliance.

We recommend that the [Disbursing Office to strictly comply  with
all applicable laws, rules and regulations in the liquidation  of  his  cash
advances to avoid  suspensions  in  audit  and  the  possibility  of  facing
problem in the future where some claimants may deny that they  were  already
paid of their claims.

We recommend  that  the  concerned  LGU  officials  and  employees  to
safeguard  government  resources  and  prevent  loss,  misappropriation  and
misuse of government funds and resources.

We recommend that the Municipal  Accountant  to  fully  implement  the
provisions of Section 1.1.4.1, COA Circular  No.  2012-001  dated  June  14,
2012 and other applicable laws, rules and regulations in  the  granting  and
utilization of cash advances.

We recommend that the concerned LGU officials  to  strictly  implement
all  applicable  rules  and  regulation  related  to  the  disbursement   of
government funds  to avoid suspensions, disallowances  in the  future  as  a
result of  illegal and unauthorized transactions.

We recommend for  the  strict  compliance  in  allocating  1%  of  the
Municipality’s Internal  Revenue  Allotment  (IRA)  as  provided  for  under
Section 15 of RA 9344 to  ensure  that  the  LGU’s  Local  Council  for  the
Protection of  Children  can  adopt  a  comprehensive  plan  on  delinquency
prevention, and to oversee its proper implementation.

We recommend that in the ensuing years, the Local Chief Executive should instruct the Municipal Budget Office to immediately submit to the SP the action taken on the findings and recommendations observed by the LFC so that the appropriation ordinance shall be declared operative in its entirety.
observations.managementAction: Complied
